arts.ttl: Kevin McDonald signs Exeter City contract extension
arts.sum: Despite injuries disrupting his time at St James Park, meaning he has only ever penned short-term contracts since first arriving in Devon last September, the 23-year-old has featured 18 times this season and started four of the last five matches. McDonald is one of three players that are on short-term deals at St James Park and the club have confirmed they are still in talks with the in-form Caleb Watts over extending his stay as well.

arts.ttl: Breaking: Birmingham City CEO Garry Cook steps down for 'personal reasons'
arts.sum: Birmingham City have announced that Garry Cook has stepped down from his position as chief executive due to personal reasons. A club statement said: "The Board has accepted Garry’s decision which he has made for personal reasons but are pleased to confirm that he has agreed to remain a member of the club’s Advisory Committee.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City Women: One out, coach in and a unique step taken
arts.sum: Bradford has joined Merricks' staff after leaving Rangers where she worked as number two to ex-Blues player and coach Jo Potter. Top-of-the-table Birmingham City Women continue their quest to return to the Super League with a trip to basement side Portsmouth this weekend.

arts.ttl: Stansfield, Bielik, Paik: Chris Davies gives bumper Birmingham City injury update
arts.sum: Davies said: “Jay Stansfield has been training and should be available, Krystian Bielik is close, absolutely, Paik has had a little bit of an issue in the week but looks good, then there’s a few just behind that.” Skipper Krystian Bielik and Paik Seung-ho could also return, leaving Blues without Tomoki Iwata, Willum Willumsson, Keshi Anderson and Emil Hansson, plus long-term absentee Lee Buchanan.

arts.ttl: Defender ‘set for medical’ at Birmingham City as Chris Davies addresses transfer need
arts.sum: Blues need to sign a new defender this month after loaning out Dion Sanderson to Blackburn Rovers and Hanley would provide competition to Christoph Klarer, Krystian Bielik and Ben Davies. Speaking about Hanley recently, Norwich boss Thorup said: "From a selfish perspective, I want to keep him because he's a leader and he's a captain, and he's a very experienced guy; he's training well, and he's good around the young boys.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City on the verge of Norwich City transfer swoop
arts.sum: Birmingham City are set to sign Norwich City’s Grant Hanley with the defender undergoing a medical, according to… The post Birmingham City on the verge of Norwich City transfer swoop appeared first on The72 - Football League News.
